pi_items.source: Verified against seed_library.py ML_PAPERS list (lines 132, 178) and web search confirming both are real papers: Vaswani et al. 2017 (NIPS) and LeCun et al. 2015 (Nature)
Verified against seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS list (line 99) and web search confirming it is Turing's 1950 paper published in Mind journal
Verified against seed_library.py CLASSIC_PAPERS list (line 51) and web search confirming it is Einstein's 1905 paper published in Annalen der Physik
Verified by analysis of verifier.py (lines 77, 82, 87) which checks for these exact tag values; setup_task.sh injects notes that the agent must convert to these tags

privileged_info_summary: The task uses four real academic papers: (1) 'Attention Is All You Need' (Vaswani et al., NIPS 2017), (2) 'Deep Learning' (LeCun, Bengio, Hinton, Nature 2015), (3) 'Computing Machinery and Intelligence' (Turing, Mind 1950), and (4) 'On the Electrodynamics of Moving Bodies' (Einstein, Annalen der Physik 1905). The data is synthetic: notes containing 'Status: Urgent' and 'Status: Later' are artificially injected into papers 1-3 via the setup_task.sh script, while paper 4 serves as a control with a 'Key reference' note. Agents must convert status notes to tags: 'priority:high' for urgent papers and 'priority:low' for later papers, while preserving control paper notes.
